resume:
Supply Chain Intern Candidate — Operations & Procurement

Profile: Final-year Operations Management student seeking a supply-chain internship; one prior summer internship in warehouse operations.

Experience (STAR):
Warehouse Operations Intern, Crestwood Distribution (Summer 2024)
- Situation: The pick-and-pack area had a 6% mis-pick rate causing return-shipment costs.
- Task: Help reduce mis-picks during my 10-week internship.
- Action: Shadowed pickers, logged the top 5 error SKUs, and proposed relocating 3 look-alike items to separate bins.
- Result: Mis-pick rate on those SKUs dropped from 6% to 2.5% over the last 3 weeks.

Projects:
- Built a small reorder-point model in a class project for a simulated retailer.

Skills: Excel, basic SQL, inventory basics, process observation, Lean intro coursework.

Education: B.S. Operations Management (expected next year).
jd: We're hiring a Supply Chain Intern to support our procurement and warehouse-operations team. You'll help with purchase-order tracking, supplier follow-ups, inventory counts, and small process-improvement projects under a senior analyst's guidance. We value honest, specific reflection over breadth: pick one project, tell us the actual problem, exactly what you did, and how you measured the result. The ideal intern can stay focused on a single example when asked and resist the urge to list everything at once. This is an entry-level role with structured mentorship and a path to a full-time offer for strong performers.

history.q: Tell me about your warehouse internship — what was the team and what were you responsible for?
Your resume says you cut mis-picks from 6% to 2.5% on certain SKUs. Pick that one project and walk me through exactly what you did.
How did you decide which SKUs were the top error drivers?
When you proposed relocating the bins, who did you bring that to, and did it actually get implemented?
Stay on the mis-pick project for a second — how did you confirm the rate actually dropped to 2.5%?
Just the mis-pick project: what was the single most important thing you personally did?

candidate_last_answer: So there were honestly a bunch of things going on that summer. Like, besides the mis-picks, I also helped a little with the inventory count, and there was this whole thing with a supplier being late that I kind of got pulled into, and I did some Excel cleanup for the inbound dock schedule too. On the mis-pick one specifically, I mean, I just kind of looked at stuff and made some suggestions and things got better. It's hard to pull out one thing because it all sort of blended together — I was helping wherever they needed me, you know?

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: It's hard to pull out one thing because it all sort of blended together

expected-yield: forcing the candidate to isolate the single mis-pick project and narrate the one concrete action he personally took and how he verified the 2.5% result, rather than blending it with the inventory count, the late supplier, and the dock-schedule cleanup
